perf_hint: each pixel replays the hits still rippling (about `4.6 / calm` seconds of them), so busy songs at low `calm` cost the most — on software rendering preview with `--sample 10s` before committing to 1080p

params.name: speed
params.description: How fast a ring expands, in frame widths per second.

params.name: calm
params.description: How quickly the water forgets a hit; higher stills it sooner.

params.name: width
params.description: How wide a young ring is, as a fraction of the short edge.

params.name: spread
params.description: How much a ring widens and softens as it travels.

params.name: scatter
params.description: How widely the strikes land; 0 drops every hit in the center.

params.name: tint
params.description: How much each strike leans the water's palette tone.

params.name: brightness
params.description: How much light the loudness of the song puts into the water.
